package repository

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for unscoped_query_without_explicit_danger_naming: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `Unscoped()` usage in generic repository methods without a clearly dangerous or admin-only name.
type CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingModel
}

type CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingService struct {
    repo *CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNaming(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingDTO) error {
    var model CaseUnscopedQueryWithoutExplicitDangerNamingModel
    db.Unscoped().Where("id = ?", input).Find(&model)
    _ = model
    return nil
}
